copays.disputeCopay.copy:
You have the right to dispute all or part of your VA copay charges. Write a letter explaining why you think the copay charges or balance amount may not be correct. To avoid late charges, you’ll need to dispute the debt within 30 days of receiving your bill.

Mail the letter, or bring it in person, to the business office or health administration office at your nearest VA medical center. If you send your dispute by mail, please include "Billing Dispute" on the mailing envelope.
copays.payBill.inPerson.bringItem2: A check or money order made payable to "VA" with your account number written on it.
copays.payBill.mail.item1: A check or money order (made payable to "U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs"),
prescription.history.transferred.youCan: Prescriptions affected by this change have a "Transferred" status. You can manage your prescriptions at these facilities using the My VA Health portal.
travelPay.claimStatus.inManualReview.rea: The mileage is not equal to or less than the calculated limit
Your travel does not meet the eligibility requirements. For detailed information about your claim, contact your local VAMC and ask for the Beneficiary Travel department.
